analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every GEMM in the block runs on the CUDA-core (SIMT) path with the Tensor Cores completely idle, and the attention kernel uses an FP32 CUTLASS fallback that spills heavily to local memory. The model is FP32 end-to-end (nn.Linear default dtype; kernel name fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned). On Blackwell, FP32 inputs route cuBLAS GEMM to the SIMT path and route SDPA to the sm80-targeted CUTLASS mem-efficient fallback rather than a Tensor-Core FlashAttention kernel. This single dtype decision is the root cause of the dominant cost: ~17 'Kernel2' GEMM launches at ~74.5k ns each, plus 3+ fmha launches at ~137k ns each.

transformation.description: Cast the attention block's activations and weights to bf16 (or, if numerics require it, enable TF32 for the matmuls only). In bf16, aten.mm.default routes to the Tensor-Core cuBLAS path (HMMA), taking tensor_core_active_pct from 0% to high, and aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default becomes eligible for the Tensor-Core FlashAttention backend, eliminating the 757,760 local-memory spills of the FP32 sm80 CUTLASS fallback. At the Aten IR level this is an insert-cast pass: wrap each aten.mm.default input and the q/k/v feeding _scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention with aten._to_copy.default(dtype=bf16), and cast back at the block output. Because this is the cheapest sweeping win and a structural prerequisite for QKV fusion (which calls register_buffer at the runtime dtype), it must run first.
transformation.location: post_grad FX graph, all aten.mm.default and aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default nodes
transformation.code_hint: Alternatively, the lowest-risk variant: leave the graph FP32 and set torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32=True + torch.backends.cudnn.allow_tf32=True so the Kernel2 GEMMs use the TF32 Tensor-Core path without an Aten-IR cast pass. bf16 is the higher-upside option and also fixes the SDPA backend selection.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Halves GEMM operand/result traffic (fp32->bf16) and eliminates the 757,760-wavefront local-memory spill stream in the attention kernel by switching from the sm80 CUTLASS fallback to a Tensor-Core attention backend.

optimizations.notes: duration_ns values are ncu-inflated (2-5x) and used here only for relative ranking. The decisive evidence is smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active=0 on every Kernel2 GEMM and the _f32_*_sm80 kernel name with 757,760 local spills. If model accuracy is sensitive, prefer TF32-only for the matmuls and keep SDPA in fp32, accepting a smaller win.

bottleneck.description: The q_proj, k_proj, and v_proj projections appear as three independent aten.mm.default nodes (op_id 4/5/6 in one block, 26/27/28 in another) all consuming the same ln_pre output and all with identical shape [4096,512]x[512,512]. Each launches a separate Kernel2 with grid [64,1,2] = 128 blocks. On a 188-SM Blackwell part, 128 blocks fills only ~0.68 of one wave, leaving SMs idle (achieved_occupancy 16.5%). Three small back-to-back GEMMs pay three launch latencies and three sub-wave launches instead of one well-shaped GEMM.

transformation.description: Concatenate the three projection weights into one [512,1536] buffer and replace the three aten.mm.default nodes with a single aten.mm.default of shape [4096,512]x[512,1536], then split the output into q/k/v via three aten.slice.Tensor views. This collapses 3 kernel launches to 1 per attention block and triples the GEMM N dimension (512->1536), producing 3x the output column tiles so the single launch covers ~2 waves and raises SM occupancy. Must run after OPT-1: the fused QKV weight is created via register_buffer, which allocates at the runtime dtype and cannot be recast after dtype promotion has run.
transformation.location: post_grad FX graph, the three sibling aten.mm.default nodes sharing the ln_pre output
fx_steps: qmm, kmm, vmm = <the three sibling aten.mm.default nodes sharing input act node>
wq, wk, wv = qmm.args[1], kmm.args[1], vmm.args[1]   # transposed weight nodes
transformation.code_hint: Pre-concatenating weights as a registered buffer once (rather than aten.cat at runtime) avoids the per-call concat cost; register the [512,1536] buffer in the pass setup after dtype promotion fixes the buffer dtype.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: The shared ln_pre activation [4096,512] is read once instead of three times; one result write instead of three. Better SM occupancy from a single ~2-wave launch vs three 0.68-wave launches.

optimizations.notes: Two QKV triplets are visible (op_id 4/5/6 and 26/27/28), confirming Inductor did NOT fuse them. Eliminated launches counted across both triplets (2 per triplet x 2 triplets = 4).

bottleneck.description: SDPA is dispatching to the memory-efficient CUTLASS backend (fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80) rather than FlashAttention. The kernel shows 757,760 local-memory spill wavefronts, registers_per_thread=168, achieved_occupancy 14%, and eligible_cycles_pct 33.9% (latency-bound by Blackwell's <20%-adjacent criterion). The transpose(1,2) on q/k/v in the source produces a non-contiguous, head-strided layout; combined with FP32, the dispatcher rejects the FlashAttention backend and falls back to the spilling sm80 mem-efficient kernel. Each launch is ~137k ns, the single largest per-launch cost in the profile.

transformation.description: After dtype promotion (OPT-1) makes q/k/v bf16 and QKV fusion (OPT-2) produces them from one GEMM, insert aten.clone with contiguous_format on the q/k/v inputs to aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default so they present the contiguous [B,H,T,D] layout the FlashAttention backend requires. With bf16 + contiguous heads, the dispatcher selects the Tensor-Core FlashAttention kernel, eliminating the 757,760-wavefront spill stream and raising occupancy. Must run after OPT-2 because the SDPA-rewrite pass walks the graph for q/k/v producer nodes, which only exist in their final form once QKV fusion + slicing has created them.
transformation.code_hint: Validate the backend switch by re-profiling: a successful switch replaces fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_*_sm80 with a flash/efficient bf16 kernel and drops l1tex local-spill wavefronts toward 0. If the dispatcher still picks mem-efficient, gate with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION) at the module level.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates the local-memory spill traffic (757,760 wavefronts per launch) by moving from the register-spilling sm80 FP32 fallback to a Tensor-Core FlashAttention kernel that keeps the attention tile in shared memory/registers.

optimizations.notes: Confidence capped at medium: web search (exa) and a local fx-patterns.md were unavailable, so the backend-selection claim is from kernel-name evidence and dispatcher heuristics, not a cited pattern. The bulk of this win is unlocked by OPT-1; this pass secures the FlashAttention path that OPT-1 makes eligible.

global_notes: FALLBACK: knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in the repo. Used built-in Blackwell limits (RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell ~ 188 SMs, GB202 class) and built-in FX surgery patterns. SM count is an assumption; wave-quantization math in OPT-2 uses 188 SMs.

Blackwell: warp_cycles_per_instruction is null across all operators (metric removed on this architecture). Used eligible_cycles_pct as the latency-bound indicator instead (fmha at 33.9% is latency/occupancy-limited).
0 operators below the 1% threshold were omitted (all 13 attributed operators are >=1% of attributed time).

DOUBLE-COUNT WARNING: the 'layer::unique::prologue' NVTX range (913,658 ns, 41% of attributed time, medium-confidence NVTX attribution) wraps the same physical kernels that the high-confidence torch_profiler ops (aten::mm_0, aten::_efficient_attention_forward_0) attribute with distinct kernel_ids. The true distinct workload is ~17 Kernel2 GEMMs (~74.5k ns each) + 3-4 fmha launches (~137k ns each) + tiny LayerNorm triton kernels. Time-budget percentages are therefore inflated by the layer wrapper; rankings rely on per-kernel counter evidence, not the wrapper total.

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count: aten::_efficient_attention_forward op_id=14 and op_id=36 have is_fused=true (fused with their _scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention parent). Their single fmha kernel is the SDPA realization; not separately optimizable beyond OPT-3.
measure_iters is absent from capture_metadata, so warm_up_inflation could not be evaluated; 9 warm-up/init kernels are already excluded via the warnings[] list.

Dependency DAG (no cycles): OPT-1 (dtype) -> OPT-2 (QKV fusion, register_buffer at runtime dtype) -> OPT-3 (SDPA layout/backend, needs final q/k/v producer nodes). Apply strictly in priority order 1,2,3.
